trivia_bullets.text: Memory Drain is original to Theros Beyond Death (January 2020) and has never been reprinted. The set's narrative centers on the Underworld — a multi-ward realm ruled by Erebos — and Phenax, god of deception, is the patron of the Returned and may hold domain over Nerono's sea.

Nerono is one of the geographic wards of the Theros Underworld: final home of souls haunted by their memories and mariners lost at sea. Vast still oceans, aimless ghost ships, and titanic algae-slick chains define the landscape. Within it lies the Labyrinth of Memories, a maze of waterways that traps travelers in an inescapable circuit for eternity. The flavor text literalizes this: Alythos sits staring over Nerono's glassy ocean, trying in vain to remember what he knew he had forgotten.
sources: WebSearch snippet: Underworld lore, Nerono ward description

trivia_bullets.text: "Alythos sat and stared blankly out over the glassy oceans of Nerono, trying in vain to remember what he knew he had forgotten." — Alythos appears to be a named soul of Nerono and does not appear elsewhere in published Theros canon as a major character.

At 4 mana for Counter target spell plus Scry 2, Memory Drain is limited-oriented and does not see competitive play above Pauper. EDHREC rank 16200 places it among the lowest-played cards in Commander. Hipsters of the Coast THB common review noted that 4-mana soft counters are generally too slow for Limited.
sources: Hipsters of the Coast: 'Every Common in Theros Beyond Death Reviewed', January 2020, via WebSearch snippet

related_cards.name: Phenax, God of Deception
related_cards.note: god of deception and patron of the Returned; Nerono falls in his sea-and-memory domain

related_cards.name: Ashiok, Nightmare Muse
related_cards.note: nightmare planeswalker allied with Phenax; mind-invasion and memory theft are Ashiok's core mechanic

related_cards.name: Labyrinth of Skophos
related_cards.note: set-mate encoding the same inescapable-confinement motif as the Labyrinth of Memories
